name: Spiced Pumpkin Soup
tagline: Roasted pumpkin and ginger purée — beta-carotene and potassium in a bowl

hero.alt: Bright orange pumpkin soup in a bowl with a swirl of cream and pumpkin seeds
hero.caption: pumpkin soup — roasted, spiced, swirled

steps.title: Roast the pumpkin
steps.detail: Toss pumpkin cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking tray with whole garlic cloves. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes till caramelised at the edges.
steps.tip: Caramelised edges are non-negotiable — that's where 80% of the flavour comes from.

steps.title: Sauté the aromatics
steps.detail: Heat remaining oil in a pot. Sauté onion till soft, add ginger and cumin powder. Cook 1 minute.
steps.tip: Don't brown the onion here — you want sweet, not roasted.

steps.title: Blend and simmer
steps.detail: Add roasted pumpkin and garlic to the pot. Pour in stock, simmer 5 minutes. Blend with a stick blender till silky. Adjust salt and pepper. Top with toasted pumpkin seeds.
steps.tip: A dollop of yogurt or coconut cream adds richness without overpowering.

healthFacts: Pumpkin: 8500µg beta-carotene per cup — converts to vitamin A in the body.
Potassium content (~560mg per cup) supports blood pressure and fluid balance.
Beta-carotene absorption needs fat — the olive oil and cream are functional, not just garnish.
Low energy density (only ~50 kcal per cup) — keeps appetite satisfied without calorie load.
